prompt: If a rectangle is 8 cm long and 5 cm wide, what is its area?

continuations.text:
To find the area of a rectangle, you multiply its length by its width. For a rectangle that is 8 cm long and 5 cm wide, the area is calculated as follows:

\[ \text{Area} = \text{Length} \times \text{Width} \]
\[ \text{Area} = 8 \, \text{cm} \times 5 \, \text{cm} \]
\[ \text{Area} = 40 \, \text{cm}^2 \]

So, the area of the rectangle is 40 square centimeters.

\[ \text{Area} = 8 \, \text{cm} \times 5 \, \text{cm} = 40 \, \text{cm}^2 \]

So, the area of the rectangle is 40 square centimeters.

vc.answer:
The area of a rectangle can be found by multiplying its length by its width. For a rectangle that is 8 cm long and 5 cm wide, the area would be:

\[ \text{Area} = \text{length} \times \text{width} = 8 \, \text{cm} \times 5 \, \text{cm} = 40 \, \text{cm}^2 \]

So, the area of the rectangle is 40 square centimeters.
